concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms United States's urban landscape. New York holds 5.0% of tracked urban population across 5344 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — New York & Philadelphia
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between New York and Philadelphia (128 km apart, combined population 24,529,004). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 128 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: United States presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with New York holding just 5.0% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (New York → Philadelphia, 128 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
